# `sesam hook pre-commit` is run by git before a commit. It re-seals any
# revealed secret whose plaintext drifted from its sealed object, verifies the
# audit log, and stages `.sesam` so the fresh ciphertext lands in the commit.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

# Init, register a secret and commit the sealed baseline.
exec sesam init --user admin
cp secret.env app.env
exec sesam add app.env --group admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam with a secret'

# Sanity: the sealed object matches the plaintext right after add.
exec sesam status --json
stdout '"in_sync"'

# Drift the plaintext. The sealed object no longer matches.
cp modified.env app.env
exec sesam status --json
stdout '"out_of_sync"'

# The hook re-seals the drift and stages the updated object.
exec sesam hook pre-commit

# The object now matches the plaintext again…
exec sesam status --json
stdout '"in_sync"'

# …and the re-sealed object is staged for the pending commit.
exec git diff --cached --name-only
stdout 'app\.env\.sesam'

# On a repo that was never initialized, the hook is a silent no-op so it never
# blocks commits in non-sesam repos.
cd $WORK
mkdir plain
cd plain
exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'
exec sesam hook pre-commit
! stdout .

-- secret.env --
TOKEN=super-secret
-- modified.env --
TOKEN=rotated-secret
